text:
Jack Draperwas left unimpressed after tennis stars at theOlympicshad no choice but to drink ‘hot water’ in the sweltering heat ofParis.

The Team GB star’s Olympics campaign came to an end on Tuesday after he was knocked out of the men’s singles.

The British No.1 won the first set against seventh seed Taylor Fritz on the tiebreak, but the American was in control afterward, winning the remaining sets, 6-3, 6-2.

Like many others, Draper struggled to cope with the scorching conditions at Roland Garros, with temperatures hitting at least 36 degree celsius in the French capital.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

The 22-year-old acknowledged it was all part of the game, but also hit out at organisers after he was unable to keep his water cool, saying: ‘It was tough conditions, there’s no doubt about it.

‘I need to play more in this sort of heat, and obviously, going toAmerica, I’ll get more used to that. It was really hot out there today.

‘We were both trying our best to put out some good tennis. Part of it’s physicality but I said to the referee, it’s pretty poor that the players have to drink hot water when we’re playing in those conditions.

‘It’s important we have good fridges to keep our water cool. It’s so tough to recover when you’re just not replenishing properly.’

Instead of fridges, players have been given refillable bottles and water fountains, with organisers looking to be more environmentally friendly and make Paris 2024 the greenest games in history.

‘There’s no escape from the heat, even at the change of ends. I haven’t played in much hotter conditions than that. Your body starts to really fight against you,’ Draper added.

‘We shouldn’t have to fill our water up every change of ends, we need to sit down. It’s not good enough. I just think it’s pretty poor that they put the players through that on court.’

He wasn’t the only tennis star left fuming on Tuesday, with the world No.2Coco Gauff left in tearsafter she was shockingly eliminated from the women’s singles.

The US flagbearer hit out at the umpire for her match, exclaiming: ‘I’m getting cheated!’

title: Team GB star fumes after having to drink ‘hot water’ during Paris heatwave | Metro News
description: Jack Draper was left unimpressed after tennis stars at the Olympics had no choice but to drink 'hot water' in the sweltering heat of Paris.
